message:
fix(fnp-python): take_along_axis / put_along_axis OOB match numpy IndexError

Both in-Rust fast paths mapped UFuncError::Msg('index N out of bounds')
via map_ufunc_error which flattens to PyValueError. numpy.take_along_axis
and numpy.put_along_axis raise IndexError — user code catching
IndexError specifically failed to catch ours.

Fix pattern (same as take in 5f28c09): on in-Rust Err, fall back to
numpy.take_along_axis / numpy.put_along_axis so the exception type,
message, and traceback match numpy exactly. The in-place variant
(put_along_axis) is safe to re-drive through numpy because our error
occurs before the write-back to the user's array, so numpy's fallback
performs the full op against the pristine input.

Added regression probes:
- tests::take_along_axis_index_out_of_bounds_matches_numpy_indexerror
- tests::put_along_axis_index_out_of_bounds_matches_numpy_indexerror
